body.title: Per-reply timing + drop telemetry on utility_closet_client — stop losing Igor's web replies silently
body.result: Per-reply telemetry shipped. _post_with_telemetry + _classify_outcome + send_message wire. Drops now post '[web_reply] ✗ session=<id> drop=<outcome> elapsed=<N>ms: <preview>' to channel. 11 new tests, 29 total. Confirmed against live drop observed 064206 this morning. d5186515
body.description:
Akien (2026-04-22): 'it's annoying to have to go find the replies in the noise.' Symptom: some Igor replies never appear in the web UI window. Working diagnosis (confirmed 2026-04-22): NOT related to the pe_chain pre-flight socket.recv timeout — pre-flight runs in a subprocess, doesn't share connection pool or event loop with the reply path. The web-reply path is its own thing: wild_igor/igor/web/utility_closet_client.py:send_message → urllib.urlopen POST to UC on port 8082, 30s timeout, WARN-on-fail (silent drop from Igor's perspective once logged).

Fix: add per-reply timing + drop telemetry so we can see WHICH replies are dropping and WHY.

1. Wrap the POST in timing: log elapsed_ms per call (INFO on success, WARN on timeout/5xx, ERROR on connection refused).
2. Include: session_id, reply-preview (first 80 chars), byte size, HTTP status (if any), elapsed_ms, outcome (delivered / timeout / 4xx / 5xx / connection_error).
3. Feed a counter into the existing proprioception ring: 'web_reply_drop' category with reason tag. Lets us see drop-rate over time in the diagnostic surface.
4. If the POST times out, DO NOT silently continue — post a channel diagnostic '[web_reply] ✗ session=<id> drop={reason}' so CC/Akien can spot the drop in real time.

Separate ticket covers the root cause of the drops (UC server load, downstream hand-off, etc.) — this one just makes drops VISIBLE so root-cause is diagnosable.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/web/utility_closet_client.py (add timing+drop telemetry around send_message), wild_igor/igor/memory/cortex.py (maybe: if proprioception write helper lives there), tests/test_utility_closet_client.py (new or existing).
**Design rules:** docs-in-code (timing-log format documented in _post docstring), test-plan-or-why-not.
**Scope boundary:** IN — timing+outcome logging, channel diagnostic on drop, proprioception counter. OUT — fixing WHY drops happen (separate follow-up ticket once we have telemetry data), UC server-side telemetry, retry logic.
**Test plan:** (a) mock urlopen success → expect INFO log with elapsed_ms + outcome=delivered; (b) mock urlopen timeout → expect WARN log + channel diagnostic post + outcome=timeout; (c) mock connection refused → expect ERROR log + drop counter increment; (d) reply preview in log is capped at 80 chars (no PII/noise bloat).
